brief_justification: Correct answer (Yes) with exact peak of 22.0°C confirmed via valid minute-by-minute trace; identifies second-order delay effect causing overshoot and classifies feedback loop correctly as balancing, tracing why lag undermines correction signal; explicitly applies injection context and notes continuous vs discrete distinction, but doesn't deeply interrogate its own assumptions mid-analysis; performs verification via systematic table but doesn't re-derive from an alternate angle; acknowledges discrete vs continuous ambiguity with ~21.5°C estimate but doesn't enumerate all modeling assumptions; mentions both discrete and continuous models with different results, briefly addresses both; complete minute-by-minute state table with event annotations allows full reproduction; explicitly labels loop as 'negative/balancing feedback' and correctly avoids the anti-pattern of treating it as reinforcing; exhibits none of the suppressed failure modes (loop blindness, momentum ignoring, linear extrapolation, reinforcing loop tolerance) and explicitly notes the balancing loop and delay-driven overshoot; follows S1→S2→S3→CLASSIFY→G1(yes→FLAG) arc but doesn't explicitly name topology stages or explore M-node branches.
